prevalence.population: Women of reproductive age in India
prevalence.trend: increasing with urbanization, obesity, and sedentary lifestyle

vulnerablePopulations: women with limited access to specialist care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Rotterdam criteria with exclusion of other causes; clinical/biochemical hyperandrogenism and ultrasound findings

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; lifestyle and medication adherence limit outcomes

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Diagnosis often in gynecology/endocrine clinics in urban centers; labs/US access limited in rural areas
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lifestyle/weight management, OCPs, metformin, fertility care when desired; screening for diabetes and dyslipidemia
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High metabolic risk and uneven access to endocrinology/fertility services
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Broad access to metformin and culturally accepted lifestyle interventions (diet/yoga) as adjuncts
